SHAP Updated: System Price ~ Marginal Identity × NIV Side — 2023–2025

Updated TreeSHAP values from one selected flex-3 RF regressor per predictor family (seed=0). SHAP decomposes each prediction into additive per-feature contributions, giving both direction and magnitude of each label×side combination's effect on predicted system price. Generated: 2026-05-04 17:09

Setup

ItemValue
Rows67,955 marginal candidate rows
Unique SPs50,684
Date range2023-01-01 to 2025-12-31
TargetsystemPrice (£/MWh) — regression
ProfileUpdated selected profile: flex-3 depth/leaf settings chosen from the runtime/stability sweep.
RF treesfuel type 300 | family 200 | BMU 200 — random_state=0, max_features=sqrt
RF regularisationmax_depth fuel 16 | family 24 | BMU 24; min_samples_leaf=3
SHAP methodTreeSHAP exact (tree_path_dependent perturbation)
SHAP rowsfuel type 10,000 | family 5,000 | BMU 2,500 (random subsample, seed=0)
EncodingLabel suffixed _OFFER or _BID before one-hot encoding — same as sided MDI script

SHAP decomposes each prediction into additive per-feature contributions around a global baseline (expected model output). For binary dummy features, read directional values as model associations: when this label-side dummy is active, the model's predicted price is pushed above or below baseline. These are not causal unit-offer experiments.

Predictor: Generation type × NIV side

37label×side combinations300trees0.366train R²80.3 £/MWhSHAP baseline

Mean SHAP — directional impact on system price

Conditional mean SHAP: average SHAP value across SPs where that label×side is actually at the margin (feature=1). Green bars push predicted price above the baseline (80.3 £/MWh) when that technology sets the margin; red bars suppress it. Ranked by mean |SHAP| (unconditional) so the most price-relevant features appear at the top.

MDI vs mean |SHAP| — importance method comparison

Both metrics normalised to max=1 for visual comparison (top 30 features by MDI). Close agreement validates the MDI ranking. Divergence highlights cases where MDI overstates importance (high split frequency but low actual price contribution) or understates it (infrequent but high-impact splits).

SHAP beeswarm — distribution of per-SP effects (top 20 features)

Each point is one settlement period (10,000 sampled). Red = feature active (this label×side is at margin); blue = feature inactive. Horizontal spread of red dots shows how price impact varies across SPs where that label×side is marginal — a wide spread indicates context-dependent or volatile pricing.
